data.title: The Reactor Core Equalizer
data.statement:
The goal is to make all values in an N × N integer grid equal using row/column increment operations, with the minimum number of operations.
Rules

One operation chooses either:

a row index i and increases every cell in row i by 1, or
a column index j and increases every cell in column j by 1.


Operations may be applied any number of times.
Values can only increase.

Task
Compute the minimum total number of operations required to make all grid values equal. If it is impossible, output -1.
data.constraints:
1 ≤ N ≤ 500
Row/column sums fit in 64‑bit integers
Time limit: suitable for O(N²)
data.stubGenerator:
read n:int
loop n read row:string(20000)
write answer
data.inputDescription:
N
<grid of N lines, each with N integers>

N is the grid size (1 ≤ N ≤ 500)
Energy levels are non‑negative integers ≤ 10⁹
data.outputDescription: A single integer — the minimum number of operations required to stabilize the reactor grid, or -1 if it's not possible.

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

The goal is to make all values in an N × N integer grid equal using row/column increment operations, with the minimum number of operations.
Rules

One operation chooses either:

a row index i and increases every cell in row i by 1, or
a column index j and increases every cell in column j by 1.


Operations may be applied any number of times.
Values can only increase.

Task
Compute the minimum total number of operations required to make all grid values equal. If it is impossible, output -1.
Input
N
<grid of N lines, each with N integers>

N is the grid size (1 ≤ N ≤ 500)
Energy levels are non‑negative integers ≤ 10⁹
Output
A single integer — the minimum number of operations required to stabilize the reactor grid, or -1 if it's not possible.
Constraints
1 ≤ N ≤ 500
Row/column sums fit in 64‑bit integers
Time limit: suitable for O(N²)
Example
Input
2
4 1
0 3
Output
-1
